standard: Final human-judgment buckets for every Experiment A flag, aligned row-by-row with flag_audit_raw.csv (which holds the sentence context and mechanical evidence). A mechanical derivation hit is a candidate, not a verdict: buckets require the sentence to semantically support the match, and genuinely ambiguous flags resolve to FABRICATED so fabrication rates stay an upper bound. Rendered into experiment_a_report.md by audit_flags.py --write-report.

verdicts.justification: '25 percent price gap relative to the leader (715.18)': (715.18 - 535.68) / 715.18 = 25.1%; both operands are pool values and the sentence names the derivation.
'496.14 maintains a healthy 30% gap below the market leader price of 715.07': (715.07 - 496.14) / 715.07 = 30.6%, within the checker's own tolerance of 30; sentence names both operands.
'515.32 ... 28 percent safety buffer' vs leader 715.07: (715.07 - 515.32) / 715.07 = 27.9%; sentence names both operands.
'Reduce spending on Search by 20%': invented action parameter with no pool basis; derivation hits are chance collisions.
Invented '60% to Margin Preservation' budget split; no pool basis.
Invented '40% to Efficiency Enhancement' split; no pool basis.
Second occurrence of the invented 60% split (checker flags per occurrence).
Second occurrence of the invented 40% split.
'Reduce budget by 20% to $X': invented parameter beside a literal unfilled placeholder.
'Increase budget by 30% to $Y': same invented pattern.
Invented ROI table Spend cell ('$100k'); the pipeline computes no revenue or ROI; the only mechanical hits are degenerate (a-0)/a collisions.
Invented ROI table Revenue cell ('$500k'). Numerically coincides with the run budget 500,000, which is not itself a pool member - coincidence, not grounding.
Invented ROI table Spend cell ('$50k'); no pool value near 50,000.
